title: Helanthium parvulum
intro: Helanthium parvulum, also known as the lesser duckweed, is a small aquatic plant belonging to the family Alismataceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been reported to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. The bioactive constituents of Helanthium parvulum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to contribute to its medicinal properties. Helanthium parvulum is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Helanthium parvulum is classified as a species of aquatic plant in the family Alismataceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Within the family Alismataceae, Helanthium parvulum is placed in the genus Helanthium. This classification places the plant within the broader group of monocots. The plant's classification also indicates its evolutionary relationships with other plants in its genus and family.
uses: Helanthium parvulum, commonly known as the lesser duckweed or American duckweed, is a plant with potential medicinal uses. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
